title: Join official lap-level race status from FastF1 to expose Safety Car/VSC/red-flag/wet-transition windows
summary: Formula 1 strategy sources explicitly note that teams pre-arm Safety Car pit windows and that wet/dry crossover moments cause abrupt stop decisions; FastF1 exposes track status, session status, and race control messages at lap/session granularity.
rationale: Formula 1 strategy sources explicitly note that teams pre-arm Safety Car pit windows and that wet/dry crossover moments cause abrupt stop decisions; FastF1 exposes track status, session status, and race control messages at lap/session granularity. The current feature set appears to infer these regimes only indirectly from tyre and lap-time fields.
implementation_hint: Build a Year+Race+LapNumber join against FastF1 `track_status`, `session_status`, and `race_control_messages`. Add features like `is_sc_or_vsc`, `laps_since_status_change`, `first_green_lap_after_sc`, `red_flag_restart_window`, `recent_wet_message`, and interactions with `TyreLife`, `Compound`, and `RaceProgress`. Validate on race-held-out folds because status regimes are race-specific.
expected_effect: Better recall on abrupt pit-call clusters, especially neutralizations and weather transitions that tyre-age heuristics miss.
risk: Race-name normalization and lap alignment can be messy; external joins can silently leak if statuses from future laps are forward-filled incorrectly.
